headline: Token economics broke out across the field as IPO pressure raised questions about future pricing.
body: Token efficiency moved into breakout alongside two new entrants, tokenomics and token usage analysis, as TechCrunch framed a possible "Tokenpocalypse" in which upcoming AI IPOs push token pricing higher [0]. Control problem and economic impact also broke out, anchored by an Economist piece on whether AI may escape human oversight [7] and a software engineer's account of LLMs eroding career value [9]. On the product side, OpenAI is reportedly building a "super app" with an internal claim that "chat is dead" [4], and a new desktop application thread calls for an official Claude Desktop on Linux [8].

cited_items.title: Is this the dawn of the Tokenpocalypse?
OpenAI is still working on that ‘super app’
Will artificial intelligence soon escape human control? - The Economist
Anthropic, please ship an official Claude Desktop for Linux
LLMs are eroding my software engineering career and I don't know what to do
